1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Routing test for skill descriptions.
3	
4	For each test utterance, score every SKILL.md by counting content-word
5	overlap with the description. Report top match per utterance and whether
6	there's a clear winner (top score > runner-up).
7	
8	Usage:
9	    python3 routing_test.py                    # default 18 utterances
10	    python3 routing_test.py --strict           # exit 1 on ties/losses
11	    python3 routing_test.py --expected NAME    # use custom expected skill
12	
13	The 18 default utterances cover the 4 local meta-skills plus 13 marketplace
14	skills, designed to detect routing collisions across the full catalog.
15	"""
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import argparse
19	import re
20	import sys
21	from pathlib import Path
22	
23	# Reuse the marketplace-validator's YAML parser — it handles folded (`>`),
24	# literal (`|`), single-quoted (`'…'`), and double-quoted (`"…"`) scalars
25	# correctly (including escape sequences). Earlier this script had a custom
26	# regex that only matched `description: >\n…` and silently skipped any
27	# single-line description, so after commit 904e11e converted the marketplace
28	# descriptions to `description: "…"`, the test loaded only ~5 of 31+ skills.
29	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent))
30	from validate import parse_frontmatter_safe  # noqa: E402
31	
32	
33	def load_skills(root: Path) -> dict[str, str]:
34	    """Load all SKILL.md frontmatter descriptions."""
35	    skills: dict[str, str] = {}
36	    for p in root.rglob("SKILL.md"):
37	        text = p.read_text()
38	        fm, _ = parse_frontmatter_safe(text)
39	        desc = fm.get("description", "").strip()
40	        if not desc:
41	            continue
42	        # Use the parent directory name as the skill identifier — matches the
43	        # `name:` field by convention but tolerates mismatches (the validator
44	        # flags those separately).
45	        skills[p.parent.name] = desc
46	    return skills
47	
48	
49	def tokens(s: str) -> set[str]:
50	    return {t for t in re.split(r"\W+", s.lower()) if len(t) > 2}
51	
52	
53	# Default utterance set: covers the 4 local meta-skills + 13 marketplace
54	# skills to detect routing collisions across the full catalog. The first
55	# 10 cover the local meta-skills. The next 8 cover marketplace skills with
56	# distinctive trigger phrases across diverse domains (skill authoring,
57	# evaluation, planning, features, web search, security, Rust). Add more as
58	# new skills ship or new routing collisions are discovered.
59	DEFAULT_UTTERANCES: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
60	    # --- Local meta-skills ---
61	    ("marketplace-validator", "lint the marketplace and check the frontmatter"),
62	    ("marketplace-validator", "is this skill valid before I commit"),
63	    ("marketplace-health", "audit the marketplace and run the pre-release health check"),
64	    ("marketplace-health", "is the marketplace healthy, check for drift"),
65	    ("ingesting-skills", "port this skill from a github url into our collection"),
66	    ("ingesting-skills", "add this skill to our marketplace, import it"),
67	    ("releasing-marketplace", "cut a release and bump the version to 0.0.2"),
68	    ("releasing-marketplace", "tag and push the new version"),
69	    ("general-critic", "review this PR and find the worst issues"),
70	    ("deep-research", "do deep research on agent skill evaluation methodology"),
71	    # --- Marketplace skills ---
72	    ("crafting-skills", "create a new agent skill for my project"),
73	    ("crafting-skills", "review this skill's description and frontmatter"),
74	    ("evaluating-skills", "write evals for this skill and grade the outputs"),
75	    ("plan-lifecycle", "plan this multi-phase project with critic checkpoints"),
76	    ("task-lifecycle", "add this small feature with verification and docs"),
77	    ("web-search", "find documentation on the open web for Rust async traits"),
78	    ("security", "audit code for OWASP top 10 vulnerabilities and secrets"),
79	    ("rust", "configuring clippy and cargo-deny for my Rust project"),
80	]
81	
82	
83	def main() -> int:
84	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__, formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter)
85	    ap.add_argument("--root", type=Path, default=Path("."), help="project root (default: cwd)")
86	    ap.add_argument("--strict", action="store_true", help="exit 1 on ties/losses")
87	    args = ap.parse_args()
88	
89	    skills = load_skills(args.root)
90	    print(f"Loaded {len(skills)} skills\n")
91	
92	    clear_wins = ties = losses = 0
93	
94	    print(f"{'Exp':<25} {'Utterance':<58} {'Top match':<25} {'Score':>5} {'2nd':>4} Result")
95	    print("-" * 130)
96	
97	    for expected, utterance in DEFAULT_UTTERANCES:
98	        utt_tokens = tokens(utterance)
99	        scores: list[tuple[int, str]] = []
100	        for name, desc in skills.items():
101	            s = len(utt_tokens & tokens(desc))
102	            if s > 0:
103	                scores.append((s, name))
104	        scores.sort(reverse=True)
105	
106	        if not scores:
107	            print(f"{expected:<25} {utterance:<58} (no matches)")
108	            losses += 1
109	            continue
110	
111	        top_score, top = scores[0]
112	        second = scores[1][0] if len(scores) > 1 else 0
113	        is_clear = top_score > second
114	        is_correct = top == expected
115	
116	        if is_correct and is_clear:
117	            result = "✓"
118	            clear_wins += 1
119	        elif is_correct:
120	            result = "TIE"
121	            ties += 1
122	        else:
123	            result = "✗"
124	            losses += 1
125	
126	        print(f"{expected:<25} {utterance:<58} {top:<25} {top_score:>5} {second:>4} {result}")
127	
128	    print(f"\n=== Routing test summary ===")
129	    print(f"  Clear wins: {clear_wins}/{len(DEFAULT_UTTERANCES)}")
130	    print(f"  Ties:       {ties}/{len(DEFAULT_UTTERANCES)}")
131	    print(f"  Losses:     {losses}/{len(DEFAULT_UTTERANCES)}")
132	
133	    if args.strict and (ties or losses):
134	        return 1
135	    return 0
136	
137	
138	if __name__ == "__main__":
139	    sys.exit(main())
140

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	r"""Word-count methodology comparison for skill descriptions.
3	
4	Reads the three "exception" skill descriptions and counts their words
5	under three different methodologies:
6	
7	  - validator:  re.findall(r"\b\w+\b", desc)        — what marketplace-validator uses
8	  - routing:    re.split(r"\W+", desc.lower()) with len > 2 filter
9	                                                      — what routing_test.py uses
10	  - split:      desc.split()                        — naive whitespace split
11	
12	Prints a markdown table suitable for inclusion in the methodology note.
13	"""
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import re
17	import sys
18	from pathlib import Path
19	
20	REPO = Path("/Users/felix/Documents/AutoPluginClaw/taches-principled-light")
21	TARGETS = [
22	    ("crafting-skills",        REPO / "skills" / "crafting-skills" / "SKILL.md"),
23	    ("test-orchestration",     REPO / "skills" / "test-orchestration" / "SKILL.md"),
24	    ("marketplace-validator",  REPO / ".agents" / "skills" / "marketplace-validator" / "SKILL.md"),
25	]
26	
27	
28	def parse_frontmatter_description(text: str) -> str:
29	    """Extract the `description:` value from a SKILL.md's YAML frontmatter.
30	
31	    Handles single-line `"…"` quoted and multi-line `>` folded scalars.
32	    """
33	    if not text.startswith("---"):
34	        return ""
35	    lines = text.splitlines()
36	    if not lines or lines[0].strip() != "---":
37	        return ""
38	    end = next((i for i in range(1, len(lines)) if lines[i].strip() == "---"), None)
39	    if end is None:
40	        return ""
41	    i = 1
42	    while i < end:
43	        s = lines[i]
44	        if not s.strip() or s.lstrip().startswith("#"):
45	            i += 1
46	            continue
47	        leading = len(s) - len(s.lstrip())
48	        key, sep, val = s.partition(":")
49	        if not sep:
50	            i += 1
51	            continue
52	        key = key.strip()
53	        if key != "description":
54	            i += 1
55	            continue
56	        val = val.strip()
57	        if val.startswith('"') and val.endswith('"') and len(val) >= 2:
58	            inner = val[1:-1]
59	            inner = inner.replace("\\\\", "\x00")
60	            inner = (
61	                inner.replace('\\"', '"')
62	                .replace("\\n", "\n")
63	                .replace("\\t", "\t")
64	                .replace("\\r", "\r")
65	                .replace("\\/", "/")
66	            )
67	            return inner.replace("\x00", "\\")
68	        if val in (">", "|", ">-", "|-", ">+", "|+"):
69	            block_indent = None
70	            chunks = []
71	            j = i + 1
72	            while j < end:
73	                row = lines[j]
74	                if not row.strip():
75	                    j += 1
76	                    continue
77	                row_lead = len(row) - len(row.lstrip())
78	                if block_indent is None:
79	                    block_indent = row_lead
80	                if row_lead < block_indent:
81	                    break
82	                chunks.append(row.lstrip() if val.startswith(">") else row[block_indent:])
83	                j += 1
84	            sep = " " if val.startswith(">") else "\n"
85	            return sep.join(chunks).strip()
86	        return val
87	    return ""
88	
89	
90	def main() -> int:
91	    rows = []
92	    for name, path in TARGETS:
93	        text = path.read_text()
94	        desc = parse_frontmatter_description(text)
95	        if not desc:
96	            print(f"[warn] no description found in {path}", file=sys.stderr)
97	            continue
98	        v = len(re.findall(r"\b\w+\b", desc))
99	        r = len({t for t in re.split(r"\W+", desc.lower()) if len(t) > 2})
100	        s = len(desc.split())
101	        rows.append((name, v, r, s))
102	    print(f"\nWord-count methodology comparison (current HEAD = 0.0.2 + eeaca8b refactor)\n")
103	    print(f"| {'Skill':24} | {'validator':>9} | {'routing':>7} | {'split':>5} |")
104	    print(f"|{'-'*26}|{'-'*11}|{'-'*9}|{'-'*7}|")
105	    for name, v, r, s in rows:
106	        print(f"| {name:24} | {v:>9} | {r:>7} | {s:>5} |")
107	    print()
108	    print("validator = re.findall(r'\\b\\w+\\b', desc)  (marketplace-validator)")
109	    print("routing   = re.split(r'\\W+', desc.lower()) with len > 2  (routing_test.py)")
110	    print("split     = desc.split()  (naive whitespace)")
111	    return 0
112	
113	
114	if __name__ == "__main__":
115	    sys.exit(main())
116

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Iteration-2: full N=18 behavioral eval runner.
3	
4	For each eval in evals.json, runs the with-skill and without-skill
5	configurations via `claude --print --output-format stream-json` and
6	captures the NDJSON transcript. Parses each transcript for:
7	  - SKILL.md read tool_use events
8	  - Skill tool invocations
9	  - final text length
10	  - total event count
11	
12	Writes per-eval behavioral_comparison.json and aggregates into
13	benchmark.json + benchmark.md at the end.
14	"""
15	from __future__ import annotations
16	
17	import json
18	import subprocess
19	import sys
20	import time
21	from pathlib import Path
22	
23	REPO = Path("/Users/felix/Documents/AutoPluginClaw/taches-principled-light")
24	SKILLS_DIR = REPO / "skills"
25	# The marketplace spans two folders: top-level `skills/` (26 skills) and
26	# `.agents/skills/` (4 skills: marketplace-health, marketplace-validator,
27	# ingesting-skills, releasing-marketplace). Both are independently
28	# discoverable by recursive agents per the Agent Skills standard. Reads
29	# of either folder count toward the "consulted a marketplace skill" signal.
30	MARKETPLACE_SKILL_DIRS = [REPO / "skills", REPO / ".agents/skills"]
31	WORKSPACE = REPO / "docs/principled/skill-evals/marketplace-routing-2026-06-22"
32	ITER_DIR = WORKSPACE / "iteration-2"
33	EVALS_FILE = WORKSPACE / "evals/evals.json"
34	CLAUDE = "/Users/felix/.local/bin/claude"
35	# Per project convention, iteration evals target the Haiku chain on the
36	# inference-gateway proxy (VPS port 3456). The default model (Opus) was
37	# hitting 529 Overloaded errors (see iteration-2/API-OVERLOAD-INCIDENT.md).
38	# The proxy is configured via ~/.claude/settings.json (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL +
39	# ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN + CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=haiku); we set --model
40	# explicitly here so the choice is visible at the subprocess call site.
41	CLAUDE_MODEL = "haiku"
42	TIMEOUT_S = 300
43	EMPTY_PROJECT = Path("/tmp/empty-claude-project")
44	REQUIRED_EVAL_KEYS = ("id", "category", "expected", "utterance")
45	
46	
47	def parse_events(text: str) -> list[dict]:
48	    """Parse NDJSON stream into list of events."""
49	    out = []
50	    for line in text.splitlines():
51	        line = line.strip()
52	        if not line:
53	            continue
54	        try:
55	            out.append(json.loads(line))
56	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
57	            pass
58	    return out
59	
60	
61	def extract_skill_md_reads(events: list[dict]) -> list[str]:
62	    """Find all Read tool_use calls on SKILL.md files."""
63	    out = []
64	    for ev in events:
65	        if ev.get("type") != "assistant":
66	            continue
67	        content = ev.get("message", {}).get("content", [])
68	        if not isinstance(content, list):
69	            continue
70	        for block in content:
71	            if not isinstance(block, dict):
72	                continue
73	            if block.get("type") == "tool_use" and block.get("name") == "Read":
74	                fp = block.get("input", {}).get("file_path", "")
75	                if fp and "SKILL.md" in fp:
76	                    out.append(fp)
77	    return out
78	
79	
80	def extract_skill_tool_invocations(events: list[dict]) -> list[str]:
81	    """Find all Skill tool invocations (anywhere in the structure)."""
82	    out = []
83	    for ev in events:
84	        if ev.get("type") != "assistant":
85	            continue
86	        content = ev.get("message", {}).get("content", [])
87	        if not isinstance(content, list):
88	            continue
89	        for block in content:
90	            if not isinstance(block, dict):
91	                continue
92	            if block.get("type") == "tool_use" and block.get("name") == "Skill":
93	                skill = block.get("input", {}).get("skill") or block.get("skill", "")
94	                if skill:
95	                    out.append(skill)
96	    return out
97	
98	
99	def preflight() -> bool:
100	    """Sanity-check that the inference gateway is reachable on Haiku before
101	    launching the full 36-run sweep. Returns True if a one-shot echo responds
102	    without any error pattern (5xx, 4xx, rate limits, gateway issues).
103	    """
104	    print(f"[iter-2] preflight: {CLAUDE} --model {CLAUDE_MODEL} 'echo ok'", flush=True)
105	    cmd = [CLAUDE, "--print", "--output-format", "stream-json",
106	           "--model", CLAUDE_MODEL, "--add-dir", str(REPO),
107	           "--dangerously-skip-permissions", "echo ok"]
108	    try:
109	        result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=60)
110	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
111	        print("[iter-2] preflight FAIL: 60s timeout (gateway unreachable?)", flush=True)
112	        return False
113	    # Scan both stdout AND stderr for any error pattern. 529/Overloaded was
114	    # the original failure mode; broaden to catch 4xx/5xx, rate limits, and
115	    # generic Error/exception strings so non-529 failures abort fast.
116	    combined = (result.stdout or "") + "\n" + (result.stderr or "")
117	    error_patterns = [
118	        "529", "Overloaded",
119	        "API Error", "4xx", "5xx",
120	        "rate limit", "rate_limit",
121	        '"error":', '"is_error":true',
122	        "Exception", "Traceback",
123	    ]
124	    api_err = any(p in combined for p in error_patterns)
125	    if result.returncode != 0 or api_err:
126	        print(f"[iter-2] preflight FAIL: rc={result.returncode} api_err={api_err}", flush=True)
127	        print((result.stderr or "")[:500], flush=True)
128	        return False
129	    print("[iter-2] preflight OK", flush=True)
130	    return True
131	
132	
133	def run_one(utterance: str, with_skill: bool, eval_dir: Path) -> dict:
134	    """Run one eval configuration. Returns parsed metrics."""
135	    cfg = "with" if with_skill else "without"
136	    out_path = eval_dir / f"{cfg}_skill.jsonl"
137	    log_path = eval_dir / f"{cfg}_skill.log"
138	
139	    add_dir = str(REPO) if with_skill else str(EMPTY_PROJECT)
140	    cmd = [
141	        CLAUDE, "--print",
142	        "--output-format", "stream-json",
143	        "--model", CLAUDE_MODEL,
144	        "--add-dir", add_dir,
145	        "--dangerously-skip-permissions",
146	        utterance,
147	    ]
148	    start = time.time()
149	    try:
150	        with open(out_path, "w") as f:
151	            result = subprocess.run(
152	                cmd,
153	                stdout=f, stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
154	                timeout=TIMEOUT_S, cwd=add_dir,
155	            )
156	        duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
157	        log_path.write_text(result.stderr.decode("utf-8", errors="replace"))
158	        status = "completed" if result.returncode == 0 else f"exit_{result.returncode}"
159	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
160	        duration_ms = int(TIMEOUT_S * 1000)
161	        log_path.write_text(f"TIMEOUT after {TIMEOUT_S}s\n")
162	        status = "truncated_timeout"
163	
164	    text = out_path.read_text() if out_path.exists() else ""
165	    events = parse_events(text)
166	    reads = extract_skill_md_reads(events)
167	    tools = extract_skill_tool_invocations(events)
168	    return {
169	        "config": cfg,
170	        "status": status,
171	        "duration_ms": duration_ms,
172	        "total_events": len(events),
173	        "skill_tool_invocations": tools,
174	        "skill_md_reads": reads,
175	        "marketplace_skill_md_reads": [
176	            p for p in reads
177	            if any(str(d) in p for d in MARKETPLACE_SKILL_DIRS)
178	        ],
179	    }
180	
181	
182	def main() -> int:
183	    ITER_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
184	    evals_data = json.loads(EVALS_FILE.read_text())
185	    if "evals" not in evals_data or not isinstance(evals_data["evals"], list):
186	        print(f"[iter-2] FATAL: {EVALS_FILE} must contain an 'evals' list", file=sys.stderr)
187	        return 2
188	    evals = evals_data["evals"]
189	    for i, ev in enumerate(evals):
190	        missing = [k for k in REQUIRED_EVAL_KEYS if k not in ev]
191	        if missing:
192	            print(f"[iter-2] FATAL: eval index {i} missing keys: {missing}", file=sys.stderr)
193	            return 2
194	    print(f"[iter-2] running {len(evals)} evals x 2 configs = {len(evals) * 2} runs")
195	    print(f"[iter-2] target model: {CLAUDE_MODEL} via inference-gateway proxy (port 3456)")
196	    print(f"[iter-2] timeout per run: {TIMEOUT_S}s, total wall budget ~{len(evals) * 2 * TIMEOUT_S // 60} min")
197	    print(f"[iter-2] workspace: {ITER_DIR}")
198	
199	    if not preflight():
200	        print("[iter-2] aborting: preflight failed; will retry when gateway recovers", file=sys.stderr)
201	        return 3
202	
203	    all_results = []
204	    for i, ev in enumerate(evals, 1):
205	        eval_id = ev["id"]
206	        utterance = ev["utterance"]
207	        eval_dir = ITER_DIR / f"eval-{eval_id}"
208	        eval_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
209	        (eval_dir / "with_skill.jsonl").write_text("")
210	        (eval_dir / "without_skill.jsonl").write_text("")
211	        print(f"\n[{i}/{len(evals)}] {eval_id} (expected={ev['expected']})")
212	        print(f"  utterance: {utterance}")
213	        with_metrics = run_one(utterance, with_skill=True, eval_dir=eval_dir)
214	        print(f"  with-skill: {with_metrics['status']} dur={with_metrics['duration_ms']}ms "
215	              f"events={with_metrics['total_events']} reads={len(with_metrics['marketplace_skill_md_reads'])}")
216	        without_metrics = run_one(utterance, with_skill=False, eval_dir=eval_dir)
217	        print(f"  without-skill: {without_metrics['status']} dur={without_metrics['duration_ms']}ms "
218	              f"events={without_metrics['total_events']} reads={len(without_metrics['marketplace_skill_md_reads'])}")
219	
220	        comparison = {
221	            "eval_id": eval_id,
222	            "utterance": utterance,
223	            "expected_skill": ev["expected"],
224	            "with_skill": with_metrics,
225	            "without_skill": without_metrics,
226	            "delta": {
227	                "marketplace_skill_md_reads_added": (
228	                    len(with_metrics["marketplace_skill_md_reads"])
229	                    - len(without_metrics["marketplace_skill_md_reads"])
230	                ),
231	            },
232	            "material_difference": (
233	                len(with_metrics["marketplace_skill_md_reads"])
234	                != len(without_metrics["marketplace_skill_md_reads"])
235	            ),
236	        }
237	        (eval_dir / "behavioral_comparison.json").write_text(
238	            json.dumps(comparison, indent=2)
239	        )
240	        all_results.append(comparison)
241	
242	    n_total = len(all_results)
243	    n_material = sum(1 for r in all_results if r["material_difference"])
244	    sum_with = sum(len(r["with_skill"]["marketplace_skill_md_reads"]) for r in all_results)
245	    sum_without = sum(len(r["without_skill"]["marketplace_skill_md_reads"]) for r in all_results)
246	    benchmark = {
247	        "iteration": 2,
248	        "scope": f"full ({n_total} of 18 evals)",
249	        "runtime": "claude-code CLI",
250	        "timeout_per_run_s": TIMEOUT_S,
251	        "results": [
252	            {
253	                "eval_id": r["eval_id"],
254	                "expected_skill": r["expected_skill"],
255	                "with_skill_score": len(r["with_skill"]["marketplace_skill_md_reads"]),
256	                "without_skill_score": len(r["without_skill"]["marketplace_skill_md_reads"]),
257	                "delta": r["delta"]["marketplace_skill_md_reads_added"],
258	                "verdict": "material_difference" if r["material_difference"] else "no_difference",
259	                "details": f"docs/.../iteration-2/eval-{r['eval_id']}/behavioral_comparison.json",
260	            }
261	            for r in all_results
262	        ],
263	        "summary": {
264	            "total_evals": n_total,
265	            "n_material_difference": n_material,
266	            "n_no_difference": n_total - n_material,
267	            "with_skill_total_signals": sum_with,
268	            "without_skill_total_signals": sum_without,
269	            "with_skill_mean_per_eval": round(sum_with / n_total, 2) if n_total else 0,
270	            "without_skill_mean_per_eval": round(sum_without / n_total, 2) if n_total else 0,
271	        },
272	        "notes": (
273	            "Iteration 2 scales the pilot to full N=18 with 180s timeout. "
274	            "Signal: marketplace SKILL.md read counts. Limitation: read-counting is "
275	            "a necessary-but-not-sufficient proxy for skill application per the "
276	            "Tessl framework (arxiv 2606.17819v1). Iteration 3 would add "
277	            "assertion-based grading (per Anthropic skill-creator's executor/grader/"
278	            "comparator/analyzer pattern)."
279	        ),
280	    }
281	    (ITER_DIR / "benchmark.json").write_text(json.dumps(benchmark, indent=2))
282	
283	    md = [
284	        f"# Iteration 2 — Marketplace Routing Behavioral Eval (full N={n_total})",
285	        "",
286	        "## Configuration",
287	        f"- Runtime: Claude Code CLI (`{CLAUDE}`)",
288	        f"- Timeout: {TIMEOUT_S}s per run",
289	        f"- Wall budget: ~{n_total * 2 * TIMEOUT_S // 60} min",
290	        "- Signal: `marketplace_skill_md_reads` count per eval",
291	        "",
292	        "## Summary",
293	        f"- Evals run: {n_total}",
294	        f"- Material differences: {n_material}/{n_total}",
295	        f"- No differences: {n_total - n_material}/{n_total}",
296	        f"- With-skill total signals: {sum_with}",
297	        f"- Without-skill total signals: {sum_without}",
298	        f"- With-skill mean per eval: {benchmark['summary']['with_skill_mean_per_eval']}",
299	        f"- Without-skill mean per eval: {benchmark['summary']['without_skill_mean_per_eval']}",
300	        "",
301	        "## Per-eval results",
302	        "",
303	        "| Eval | Expected | With-skill | Without-skill | Delta | Verdict |",
304	        "|------|----------|-----------:|--------------:|------:|---------|",
305	    ]
306	    for r in benchmark["results"]:
307	        md.append(
308	            f"| {r['eval_id']} | {r['expected_skill']} | "
309	            f"{r['with_skill_score']} | {r['without_skill_score']} | "
310	            f"{r['delta']:+d} | {r['verdict']} |"
311	        )
312	    md.extend([
313	        "",
314	        "## Methodology notes",
315	        "",
316	        "- **Signal strength:** read-counting measures consultation, not application.",
317	        "  A skill read but not applied would still show as a positive signal. The",
318	        "  Tessl framework (arxiv 2606.17819v1) and Anthropic's skill-creator evals",
319	        "  instead use assertion-based grading of the final output.",
320	        "- **Iteration 3 scope:** write `assertions[]` per eval (Anthropic pattern)",
321	        "  and grade the final response text against them with an LLM-as-judge.",
322	        "",
323	    ])
324	    (ITER_DIR / "benchmark.md").write_text("\n".join(md))
325	
326	    print(f"\n[iter-2] done. {n_material}/{n_total} evals show material_difference.")
327	    print(f"[iter-2] aggregate: with={sum_with} without={sum_without} delta={sum_with - sum_without}")
328	    return 0
329	
330	
331	if __name__ == "__main__":
332	    sys.exit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Iteration-3 orchestrator: runs grader.py for each (eval, config) pair and
3	aggregates with-skill vs without-skill deltas.
4	
5	Mirrors run_iteration_2.py's structure but replaces the read-counting
6	metrics with assertion-based grading. The comparator + analyzer logic
7	lives inline here for iter-3 (defer split to separate files in iter-3.1
8	per the project's anti-abstraction principle: don't extract until
9	3+ consumers).
10	
11	Inputs (per eval, from iteration-3/assertions/<eval_id>.json):
12	  assertions[] with text + type + category + points summing to 100
13	  per category; weight override; reference_solution
14	
15	Outputs (per eval, in iteration-3/eval-<id>/):
16	  grading_with_skill.json
17	  grading_without_skill.json
18	  comparison.json (per-eval deltas + verdict)
19	
20	Aggregate outputs (in iteration-3/):
21	  benchmark.json — full results
22	  benchmark.md — human-readable report
23	"""
24	from __future__ import annotations
25	
26	import json
27	import subprocess
28	import sys
29	from pathlib import Path
30	
31	REPO = Path("/Users/felix/Documents/AutoPluginClaw/taches-principled-light")
32	WORKSPACE = REPO / "docs/principled/skill-evals/marketplace-routing-2026-06-22"
33	ITER_DIR = WORKSPACE / "iteration-3"
34	ASSERTIONS_DIR = ITER_DIR / "assertions"
35	GRADER = ITER_DIR / "scripts/grader.py"
36	TIMEOUT_S = 300
37	DEFAULT_JUDGE_MODEL = "sonnet"  # override with --judge-model
38	LIFT_THRESHOLD_PP = 5  # per Tessl Table 4 norms; matches marketplace material_difference
39	
40	
41	def run_grader(eval_id: str, config: str, transcript_path: Path,
42	               output_dir: Path, judge_model: str = DEFAULT_JUDGE_MODEL) -> dict | None:
43	    """Invoke grader.py for one (eval, config) pair. Returns parsed grading
44	    or None on failure.
45	
46	    transcript_path: where the with/without-skill NDJSON is read from
47	                     (typically iteration-2/eval-<id>/<config>_skill.jsonl)
48	    output_dir:       where grading_<config>_skill.json is written
49	                     (typically iteration-3/eval-<id>/)
50	    """
51	    assertions_path = ASSERTIONS_DIR / f"{eval_id}.json"
52	    if not assertions_path.exists():
53	        print(f"[iter-3] FATAL: no assertions for {eval_id} at {assertions_path}", file=sys.stderr)
54	        return None
55	    if not transcript_path.exists():
56	        print(f"[iter-3] {eval_id} {config}: no transcript at {transcript_path}; skipping",
57	              file=sys.stderr)
58	        return None
59	    cmd = [
60	        sys.executable, str(GRADER),
61	        "--assertions", str(assertions_path),
62	        "--transcript", str(transcript_path),
63	        "--eval-dir", str(output_dir),
64	        "--config", config,
65	        "--judge-model", judge_model,
66	    ]
67	    try:
68	        result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=TIMEOUT_S)
69	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
70	        print(f"[iter-3] {eval_id} {config}: grader TIMEOUT after {TIMEOUT_S}s", file=sys.stderr)
71	        return None
72	    if result.returncode != 0:
73	        print(f"[iter-3] {eval_id} {config}: grader rc={result.returncode}",
74	              file=sys.stderr)
75	        print((result.stderr or "")[:500], file=sys.stderr)
76	        return None
77	    out_path = output_dir / f"grading_{config}_skill.json"
78	    if not out_path.exists():
79	        return None
80	    return json.loads(out_path.read_text())
81	
82	
83	def compute_deltas(with_grading: dict, without_grading: dict) -> dict:
84	    """Compute Tessl-style deltas + verdict classification."""
85	    s_with = with_grading["summary"]
86	    s_without = without_grading["summary"]
87	    if_delta = round(s_with["instruction_following_score"] - s_without["instruction_following_score"], 1)
88	    gc_delta = round(s_with["goal_completion_score"] - s_without["goal_completion_score"], 1)
89	    overall_delta = round(s_with["overall_score"] - s_without["overall_score"], 1)
90	    # Verdict classification
91	    if abs(overall_delta) <= LIFT_THRESHOLD_PP:
92	        verdict = "skill_neutral"
93	    elif if_delta > 5 and abs(gc_delta) <= 2:
94	        verdict = "skill_lifts_quality"
95	    elif overall_delta < -LIFT_THRESHOLD_PP:
96	        verdict = "skill_hurts"
97	    elif if_delta <= 1 and gc_delta <= 1:
98	        verdict = "skill_redundant"
99	    else:
100	        verdict = "skill_lifts_quality" if overall_delta > 0 else "skill_neutral"
101	    return {
102	        "with_skill": {
103	            "instruction_following_score": s_with["instruction_following_score"],
104	            "goal_completion_score": s_with["goal_completion_score"],
105	            "overall_score": s_with["overall_score"],
106	            "passed": s_with["passed"], "failed": s_with["failed"],
107	            "unknown": s_with["unknown"], "total": s_with["total"],
108	        },
109	        "without_skill": {
110	            "instruction_following_score": s_without["instruction_following_score"],
111	            "goal_completion_score": s_without["goal_completion_score"],
112	            "overall_score": s_without["overall_score"],
113	            "passed": s_without["passed"], "failed": s_without["failed"],
114	            "unknown": s_without["unknown"], "total": s_without["total"],
115	        },
116	        "delta": {
117	            "instruction_following_delta": if_delta,
118	            "goal_completion_delta": gc_delta,
119	            "overall_delta": overall_delta,
120	        },
121	        "verdict": verdict,
122	    }
123	
124	
125	def main() -> int:
126	    import argparse
127	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
128	    parser.add_argument("--judge-model", default=DEFAULT_JUDGE_MODEL)
129	    parser.add_argument("--evals", nargs="*", default=None,
130	                        help="Subset of eval IDs to run; default = all 18")
131	    parser.add_argument("--iter2-dir", default=None,
132	                        help="Path to iter-2 eval directories; default = "
133	                             "WORKSPACE/iteration-2/eval-<id>/")
134	    args = parser.parse_args()
135	
136	    evals_data = json.loads((WORKSPACE / "evals/evals.json").read_text())
137	    evals = evals_data.get("evals", [])
138	    if args.evals:
139	        evals = [e for e in evals if e["id"] in set(args.evals)]
140	    iter2_root = Path(args.iter2_dir) if args.iter2_dir else (WORKSPACE / "iteration-2")
141	
142	    print(f"[iter-3] running grader for {len(evals)} evals x 2 configs = {len(evals) * 2} grading calls")
143	    print(f"[iter-3] judge model: {args.judge_model}")
144	    print(f"[iter-3] reading transcripts from: {iter2_root}")
145	    print(f"[iter-3] writing outputs to: {ITER_DIR}")
146	
147	    all_comparisons = []
148	    for i, ev in enumerate(evals, 1):
149	        eval_id = ev["id"]
150	        output_dir = ITER_DIR / f"eval-{eval_id}"
151	        output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
152	        iter2_eval_dir = iter2_root / f"eval-{eval_id}"
153	        print(f"\n[{i}/{len(evals)}] {eval_id} (expected={ev['expected']})")
154	        with_grading = run_grader(
155	            eval_id, "with",
156	            transcript_path=iter2_eval_dir / "with_skill.jsonl",
157	            output_dir=output_dir,
158	            judge_model=args.judge_model,
159	        )
160	        if with_grading:
161	            s = with_grading["summary"]
162	            print(f"  with-skill:    overall={s['overall_score']} IF={s['instruction_following_score']} "
163	                  f"GC={s['goal_completion_score']} P/F/U={s['passed']}/{s['failed']}/{s['unknown']}")
164	        without_grading = run_grader(
165	            eval_id, "without",
166	            transcript_path=iter2_eval_dir / "without_skill.jsonl",
167	            output_dir=output_dir,
168	            judge_model=args.judge_model,
169	        )
170	        if without_grading:
171	            s = without_grading["summary"]
172	            print(f"  without-skill: overall={s['overall_score']} IF={s['instruction_following_score']} "
173	                  f"GC={s['goal_completion_score']} P/F/U={s['passed']}/{s['failed']}/{s['unknown']}")
174	        if not (with_grading and without_grading):
175	            print(f"  SKIP comparison (missing one or both gradings)", file=sys.stderr)
176	            continue
177	        deltas = compute_deltas(with_grading, without_grading)
178	        comparison = {
179	            "eval_id": eval_id,
180	            "utterance": ev["utterance"],
181	            "expected_skill": ev["expected"],
182	            "category_class": ev.get("category", "?"),
183	            **deltas,
184	        }
185	        (output_dir / "comparison.json").write_text(json.dumps(comparison, indent=2))
186	        d = deltas["delta"]
187	        print(f"  delta: IF={d['instruction_following_delta']:+5.1f} "
188	              f"GC={d['goal_completion_delta']:+5.1f} "
189	              f"overall={d['overall_delta']:+5.1f} → {deltas['verdict']}")
190	        all_comparisons.append(comparison)
191	
192	    if not all_comparisons:
193	        print("[iter-3] no comparisons produced", file=sys.stderr)
194	        return 1
195	
196	    # Aggregate
197	    n = len(all_comparisons)
198	    mean_if = round(sum(c["delta"]["instruction_following_delta"] for c in all_comparisons) / n, 2)
199	    mean_gc = round(sum(c["delta"]["goal_completion_delta"] for c in all_comparisons) / n, 2)
200	    mean_overall = round(sum(c["delta"]["overall_delta"] for c in all_comparisons) / n, 2)
201	    verdict_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
202	    for c in all_comparisons:
203	        verdict_counts[c["verdict"]] = verdict_counts.get(c["verdict"], 0) + 1
204	    per_skill_lift: dict[str, list[float]] = {}
205	    for c in all_comparisons:
206	        per_skill_lift.setdefault(c["expected_skill"], []).append(c["delta"]["overall_delta"])
207	    per_skill_mean = {
208	        skill: round(sum(v) / len(v), 2)
209	        for skill, v in per_skill_lift.items()
210	    }
211	    benchmark = {
212	        "iteration": 3,
213	        "scope": f"{n} of 18 evals",
214	        "judge_model": args.judge_model,
215	        "results": all_comparisons,
216	        "summary": {
217	            "total_evals": n,
218	            "mean_instruction_following_delta": mean_if,
219	            "mean_goal_completion_delta": mean_gc,
220	            "mean_overall_delta": mean_overall,
221	            "verdict_distribution": verdict_counts,
222	            "per_skill_lift": per_skill_mean,
223	        },
224	        "notes": (
225	            "Iteration 3 uses assertion-based grading per the Tessl framework "
226	            "(arxiv 2606.17819v1). Judge model = "
227	            f"{args.judge_model} (proxy alias; see iteration-3-design.md for "
228	            "the bias mitigation rationale). Solver model = haiku chain. "
229	            "Lift threshold for skill_lifts_quality = +5pp (matches "
230	            "evaluating-skills' material_difference rule)."
231	        ),
232	    }
233	    (ITER_DIR / "benchmark.json").write_text(json.dumps(benchmark, indent=2))
234	
235	    # Human-readable report
236	    md = [
237	        f"# Iteration 3 — Assertion-Based Skill Grading (N={n})",
238	        "",
239	        "## Configuration",
240	        f"- Judge model: `{args.judge_model}`",
241	        f"- Solver model: haiku (project convention)",
242	        f"- Lift threshold: ±{LIFT_THRESHOLD_PP}pp for skill_neutral",
243	        "",
244	        "## Summary",
245	        f"- Evals: {n}",
246	        f"- Mean IF delta: {mean_if:+.2f}",
247	        f"- Mean GC delta: {mean_gc:+.2f}",
248	        f"- Mean overall delta: {mean_overall:+.2f}",
249	        "",
250	        f"### Verdict distribution",
251	    ]
252	    for verdict, count in sorted(verdict_counts.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
253	        md.append(f"- `{verdict}`: {count}/{n}")
254	    md.extend([
255	        "",
256	        f"### Per-skill mean overall delta",
257	        "",
258	        "| Skill | Mean Δ | Evals |",
259	        "|-------|-------:|------:|",
260	    ])
261	    for skill, lift in sorted(per_skill_mean.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
262	        md.append(f"| {skill} | {lift:+.2f} | {len(per_skill_lift[skill])} |")
263	    md.extend([
264	        "",
265	        "## Per-eval results",
266	        "",
267	        "| Eval | Expected | IF Δ | GC Δ | Overall Δ | Verdict |",
268	        "|------|----------|-----:|-----:|----------:|---------|",
269	    ])
270	    for c in all_comparisons:
271	        d = c["delta"]
272	        md.append(
273	            f"| {c['eval_id']} | {c['expected_skill']} | "
274	            f"{d['instruction_following_delta']:+5.1f} | "
275	            f"{d['goal_completion_delta']:+5.1f} | "
276	            f"{d['overall_delta']:+5.1f} | {c['verdict']} |"
277	        )
278	    md.extend([
279	        "",
280	        "## Methodology",
281	        "",
282	        "- Tessl-style per-category 0-100 scoring (IF + GC, weighted 50/50 by default)",
283	        "- Two assertions per category minimum; some evals use weight overrides",
284	        "- Code-based checks (consultation, structure with compare_args) bypass the LLM",
285	        "- UNKNOWN verdicts (judge couldn't determine) are treated as FAIL for scoring",
286	        "  but logged in `iteration-3/unknowns.md` for human review",
287	        "",
288	    ])
289	    (ITER_DIR / "benchmark.md").write_text("\n".join(md))
290	
291	    print(f"\n[iter-3] done. {n} evals graded.")
292	    print(f"[iter-3] mean overall delta: {mean_overall:+.2f}")
293	    print(f"[iter-3] verdict distribution: {verdict_counts}")
294	    return 0
295	
296	
297	if __name__ == "__main__":
298	    sys.exit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Iteration-3 grader: LLM-as-judge runner.
3	
4	For each (eval, config) pair, grades the eval's assertions[] against the
5	agent's transcript. Per the iter-3 design (iteration-3-design.md), code-based
6	assertions (consultation, structure) bypass the LLM call entirely; only
7	compliance and quality assertions invoke the judge.
8	
9	Inputs:
10	  --assertions  Path to assertions.json for this eval
11	  --transcript  Path to with_skill.jsonl or without_skill.jsonl
12	  --eval-dir    Directory where outputs are written
13	  --config      "with" or "without" (for output filename)
14	  --judge-model Alias to use as judge (default: sonnet -> MiniMax-M3)
15	
16	Outputs (in eval-dir/):
17	  grading_iter3.json — the new schema documented in iteration-3-design.md
18	"""
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import argparse
22	import json
23	import subprocess
24	import sys
25	from pathlib import Path
26	from typing import Literal
27	
28	# Per the iter-3 design, judge model is the proxy 'sonnet' alias (= MiniMax-M3),
29	# a different underlying family from the 'haiku' solver. The 'glm-5.2' alias
30	# is a stronger alternative (Intelligence Index v4.1 score 51 vs 44 for
31	# MiniMax-M3 per Simon Willison's June 17 2026 review) but is deferred to
32	# iter-3.1 calibration. Override via --judge-model glm-5.2 to test.
33	DEFAULT_JUDGE_MODEL = "sonnet"
34	PROXY_BASE_URL = "http://100.80.231.128:3456"
35	JUDGE_PROMPT = """\
36	You are an evaluator grading an AI agent's run against a list of assertions.
37	
38	You will be given:
39	1. A list of assertions (compliance/quality only — code-based checks have
40	   already been applied and are not in your scope).
41	2. The agent's transcript (NDJSON, parsed for you below).
42	3. The relevant skill's SKILL.md (if applicable).
43	4. A reference_solution describing what a complete passing response looks like.
44	
45	Your job: for each assertion, determine whether the agent satisfied it.
46	Respond ONLY with valid JSON, no markdown fences.
47	
48	Schema:
49	{{
50	  "expectations": [
51	    {{"id": "<assertion_id>", "text": "<assertion_text>",
52	     "passed": true|false, "evidence": "<one-sentence justification>",
53	     "points_awarded": <0|points>, "unknown": false}}
54	  ]
55	}}
56	
57	Rules:
58	- `unknown: true` is reserved for evidence that is genuinely ambiguous
59	  (not just uncertain — you cannot tell from the transcript). When in
60	  doubt, set `unknown: false` and grade based on the strongest signal.
61	- `evidence` should cite specific transcript events or output text.
62	- `points_awarded` is the full points value if passed, else 0.
63	
64	Assertions:
65	{assertions}
66	
67	Reference solution (what a complete passing response looks like):
68	{reference_solution}
69	
70	Transcript (relevant excerpts):
71	{transcript_excerpt}
72	"""
73	
74	
75	def parse_events(text: str) -> list[dict]:
76	    out: list[dict] = []
77	    for line in text.splitlines():
78	        line = line.strip()
79	        if not line:
80	            continue
81	        try:
82	            out.append(json.loads(line))
83	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
84	            pass
85	    return out
86	
87	
88	def extract_transcript_excerpt(events: list[dict], max_chars: int = 8000) -> str:
89	    """Return a compact textual excerpt: assistant text + tool_use summary."""
90	    lines: list[str] = []
91	    total = 0
92	    for ev in events:
93	        if total >= max_chars:
94	            lines.append("... [truncated]")
95	            break
96	        ev_type = ev.get("type", "?")
97	        if ev_type == "assistant":
98	            content = ev.get("message", {}).get("content", [])
99	            if not isinstance(content, list):
100	                continue
101	            for block in content:
102	                if not isinstance(block, dict):
103	                    continue
104	                btype = block.get("type")
105	                if btype == "text":
106	                    text = block.get("text", "")
107	                    if text.strip():
108	                        lines.append(f"[assistant] {text[:500]}")
109	                        total += len(text[:500])
110	                elif btype == "tool_use":
111	                    name = block.get("name", "?")
112	                    inp = json.dumps(block.get("input", {}))[:300]
113	                    lines.append(f"[tool_use] {name}({inp})")
114	                    total += len(name) + len(inp) + 20
115	        elif ev_type == "user":
116	            content = ev.get("message", {}).get("content", [])
117	            if isinstance(content, list):
118	                for block in content:
119	                    if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_result":
120	                        out = str(block.get("content", ""))[:300]
121	                        lines.append(f"[tool_result] {out}")
122	                        total += len(out) + 20
123	    return "\n".join(lines)
124	
125	
126	def grade_code_based_assertion(assertion: dict, events: list[dict],
127	                               expected_skill: str = "") -> tuple[bool, str, int] | None:
128	    """Grade consultation + structure assertions without an LLM call.
129	
130	    consultation: count Read on the EXPECTED SKILL.md + Skill tool invocations
131	                  of the EXPECTED skill. Spurious matches on plugin skills
132	                  (e.g., superpowers:writing-skills) are rejected. The
133	                  expected skill is taken from the assertion's `expected_paths`
134	                  field if present, else from the parent file's `expected_skill`.
135	    structure:    if compare_args set, parse Bash tool_use args and match;
136	                  otherwise return None to signal "needs LLM judge fallback".
137	    """
138	    atype = assertion.get("type")
139	    points = assertion.get("points", 0)
140	    text = assertion.get("text", "")
141	    tool_uses: list[tuple[str, dict]] = [
142	        (b.get("name", ""), b.get("input", {}))
143	        for ev in events if ev.get("type") == "assistant"
144	        for b in (ev.get("message", {}).get("content", []) or [])
145	        if isinstance(b, dict) and b.get("type") == "tool_use"
146	    ]
147	    if atype == "consultation":
148	        read_paths = [inp.get("file_path", "") for n, inp in tool_uses if n == "Read"]
149	        skill_calls = [inp.get("skill", "") for n, inp in tool_uses if n == "Skill"]
150	
151	        per_assertion_paths = assertion.get("expected_paths") or []
152	        expected_paths = list(per_assertion_paths)
153	        if not expected_paths and expected_skill:
154	            expected_paths = [
155	                f".agents/skills/{expected_skill}/SKILL.md",
156	                f"skills/{expected_skill}/SKILL.md",
157	            ]
158	        expected_skill_names = [expected_skill] if expected_skill else []
159	
160	        def _matches_expected(path: str) -> bool:
161	            if not expected_paths:
162	                return False
163	            return any(ep in path for ep in expected_paths)
164	
165	        def _skill_call_matches(name: str) -> bool:
166	            if not expected_skill_names:
167	                return False
168	            return any(en in name for en in expected_skill_names)
169	
170	        matched_reads = [p for p in read_paths if _matches_expected(p)]
171	        matched_skills = [s for s in skill_calls if _skill_call_matches(s)]
172	        if matched_reads or matched_skills:
173	            evidence = f"agent read {', '.join(matched_reads)[:200]}; Skill tool: {', '.join(matched_skills)[:200]}"
174	            return True, evidence, points
175	        return False, (
176	            f"agent did not read or invoke expected skill "
177	            f"'{expected_skill}' (paths={expected_paths}, observed_reads={read_paths[:3]}, observed_skills={skill_calls[:3]})"
178	        ), 0
179	    if atype == "structure":
180	        compare_args = assertion.get("compare_args")
181	        if compare_args:
182	            # Find Bash tool_use; verify each named arg matches assertion's expected value
183	            # (The expected value is implicit in the assertion text for now;
184	            # a richer schema would encode it. We pass if at least one Bash call has
185	            # all compare_args keys present and non-empty.)
186	            for name, inp in tool_uses:
187	                if name == "Bash":
188	                    if all(inp.get(k) for k in compare_args):
189	                        evidence = f"Bash call has args {compare_args}: {json.dumps(inp)[:200]}"
190	                        return True, evidence, points
191	            return False, f"no Bash call had all of {compare_args}", 0
192	        # No compare_args → fall back to LLM judge
193	        return None, "structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge", 0
194	    return None, f"unknown assertion type {atype!r} for code grader; falls back to LLM judge", 0
195	
196	
197	def grade_with_judge(assertions: list[dict], transcript_excerpt: str,
198	                     reference_solution: str, judge_model: str) -> list[dict]:
199	    """Invoke the judge LLM for compliance/quality assertions."""
200	    judge_assertions = [
201	        a for a in assertions if a.get("type") in ("compliance", "quality")
202	    ]
203	    if not judge_assertions:
204	        return []
205	    prompt = JUDGE_PROMPT.format(
206	        assertions=json.dumps(judge_assertions, indent=2),
207	        reference_solution=reference_solution,
208	        transcript_excerpt=transcript_excerpt[:8000],
209	    )
210	    cmd = [
211	        "curl", "-sS", "--max-time", "180",
212	        f"{PROXY_BASE_URL}/v1/chat/completions",
213	        "-H", "Authorization: Bearer dummy",
214	        "-H", "Content-Type: application/json",
215	        "-d", json.dumps({
216	            "model": judge_model,
217	            "max_tokens": 4000,
218	            "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
219	        }),
220	    ]
221	    try:
222	        result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=180)
223	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
224	        return [
225	            {"id": a["id"], "text": a.get("text", ""),
226	             "passed": False, "evidence": "judge call timed out (180s)",
227	             "points_awarded": 0, "unknown": True}
228	            for a in judge_assertions
229	        ]
230	    try:
231	        resp = json.loads(result.stdout)
232	        content = resp["choices"][0]["message"]["content"] or ""
233	    except (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, IndexError) as e:
234	        return [
235	            {"id": a["id"], "text": a.get("text", ""),
236	             "passed": False, "evidence": f"judge parse error: {e!r}",
237	             "points_awarded": 0, "unknown": True}
238	            for a in judge_assertions
239	        ]
240	    # Extract JSON from response (judges sometimes wrap in fences)
241	    content = content.strip()
242	    if content.startswith("```"):
243	        content = content.split("```", 2)[1]
244	        if content.startswith("json"):
245	            content = content[4:]
246	    try:
247	        judge_output = json.loads(content)
248	        expectations = judge_output.get("expectations", [])
249	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
250	        return [
251	            {"id": a["id"], "text": a.get("text", ""),
252	             "passed": False, "evidence": f"judge returned non-JSON: {content[:200]!r}",
253	             "points_awarded": 0, "unknown": True}
254	            for a in judge_assertions
255	        ]
256	    # Map by id; fill missing with UNKNOWN. If judge returned passed=null or
257	    # not a bool, treat as unknown (caller should review in unknowns.md).
258	    out_by_id = {e.get("id"): e for e in expectations if e.get("id")}
259	    out: list[dict] = []
260	    for a in judge_assertions:
261	        je = out_by_id.get(a["id"])
262	        if not je:
263	            out.append({
264	                "id": a["id"], "text": a.get("text", ""),
265	                "passed": False, "evidence": "no judge output for this assertion",
266	                "points_awarded": 0, "unknown": True,
267	            })
268	            continue
269	        passed_val = je.get("passed")
270	        is_unknown = not isinstance(passed_val, bool)
271	        out.append({
272	            "id": a["id"],
273	            "text": a.get("text", ""),
274	            "passed": False if is_unknown else passed_val,
275	            "evidence": je.get("evidence", "no judge output for this assertion"),
276	            "points_awarded": (je.get("points_awarded", 0) if passed_val is True else 0),
277	            "unknown": is_unknown or bool(je.get("unknown", False)),
278	        })
279	    return out
280	
281	
282	def compute_summary(expectations: list[dict], assertions: list[dict],
283	                   weight: dict[str, float]) -> dict:
284	    """Compute per-category and overall scores via Tessl-style weighted average."""
285	    by_category: dict[str, list[tuple[int, int]]] = {}
286	    for a in assertions:
287	        cat = a.get("category", "?")
288	        # Find matching expectation
289	        exp = next((e for e in expectations if e.get("id") == a.get("id")), None)
290	        awarded = exp.get("points_awarded", 0) if exp else 0
291	        by_category.setdefault(cat, []).append((awarded, a.get("points", 0)))
292	    category_scores: dict[str, float] = {}
293	    for cat, pairs in by_category.items():
294	        total_awarded = sum(p[0] for p in pairs)
295	        total_possible = sum(p[1] for p in pairs)
296	        category_scores[cat] = (
297	            round(100 * total_awarded / total_possible, 1)
298	            if total_possible else 0.0
299	        )
300	    # Weighted average across reward_basis (default: both)
301	    reward_basis = list(by_category.keys())
302	    w_sum = sum(weight.get(c, 1.0) for c in reward_basis)
303	    if w_sum == 0:
304	        overall = 0.0
305	    else:
306	        overall = round(
307	            100 * sum(category_scores[c] * weight.get(c, 1.0) for c in reward_basis) / w_sum
308	            / 100, 1
309	        )
310	    n_passed = sum(1 for e in expectations if e.get("passed") and not e.get("unknown"))
311	    n_failed = sum(1 for e in expectations if not e.get("passed") and not e.get("unknown"))
312	    n_unknown = sum(1 for e in expectations if e.get("unknown"))
313	    return {
314	        "instruction_following_score": category_scores.get("instruction_following", 0.0),
315	        "goal_completion_score": category_scores.get("goal_completion", 0.0),
316	        "overall_score": overall,
317	        "passed": n_passed, "failed": n_failed,
318	        "unknown": n_unknown, "total": len(expectations),
319	    }
320	
321	
322	def main() -> int:
323	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
324	    parser.add_argument("--assertions", required=True, type=Path)
325	    parser.add_argument("--transcript", required=True, type=Path)
326	    parser.add_argument("--eval-dir", required=True, type=Path)
327	    parser.add_argument("--config", choices=["with", "without"], required=True)
328	    parser.add_argument("--judge-model", default=DEFAULT_JUDGE_MODEL)
329	    args = parser.parse_args()
330	
331	    assertions_data = json.loads(args.assertions.read_text())
332	    assertions = assertions_data.get("assertions", [])
333	    weight = assertions_data.get("weight", {
334	        "instruction_following": 1.0, "goal_completion": 1.0,
335	    })
336	
337	    transcript_text = args.transcript.read_text() if args.transcript.exists() else ""
338	    events = parse_events(transcript_text)
339	    transcript_excerpt = extract_transcript_excerpt(events)
340	
341	    # Code-based grading (consultation + structure-with-compare_args only;
342	    # structure without compare_args returns None and falls through to judge)
343	    expected_skill = assertions_data.get("expected_skill", "")
344	    expectations: list[dict] = []
345	    fallback_to_judge: list[dict] = []
346	    for a in assertions:
347	        if a.get("grader") != "code":
348	            continue
349	        result = grade_code_based_assertion(a, events, expected_skill=expected_skill)
350	        if result is None:
351	            fallback_to_judge.append(a)
352	            continue
353	        passed, evidence, awarded = result
354	        expectations.append({
355	            "id": a["id"], "text": a.get("text", ""),
356	            "passed": passed, "evidence": evidence,
357	            "points_awarded": awarded, "unknown": False,
358	        })
359	
360	    # Model-based grading (one batched call per (eval, config))
361	    judge_pool = fallback_to_judge + [
362	        a for a in assertions if a.get("grader") == "model"
363	    ]
364	    judge_expectations = grade_with_judge(
365	        judge_pool, transcript_excerpt,
366	        assertions_data.get("reference_solution", ""),
367	        args.judge_model,
368	    )
369	    expectations.extend(judge_expectations)
370	
371	    summary = compute_summary(expectations, assertions, weight)
372	    out = {
373	        "judge_model": args.judge_model,
374	        "config": args.config,
375	        "eval_id": assertions_data.get("eval_id", args.eval_dir.name),
376	        "expectations": expectations,
377	        "summary": summary,
378	        "weight": weight,
379	    }
380	    args.eval_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
381	    out_path = args.eval_dir / f"grading_{args.config}_skill.json"
382	    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(out, indent=2))
383	    print(f"[grader] {out_path}: overall={summary['overall_score']} "
384	          f"IF={summary['instruction_following_score']} "
385	          f"GC={summary['goal_completion_score']} "
386	          f"P/F/U={summary['passed']}/{summary['failed']}/{summary['unknown']}",
387	          flush=True)
388	    return 0
389	
390	
391	if __name__ == "__main__":
392	    sys.exit(main())
393

3	Reproduces the iter-3.1 bug where timeouts left empty eval dirs
4	with no run.jsonl marker, making post-hoc analysis ambiguous
5	between "ran to completion but produced nothing" and "timed out".

8

22	def _load_module(monkeypatch, claude_shim: Path):

24	    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(
25	        "run_per_skill_experiment", SCRIPT_PATH

28	    sys.modules["run_per_skill_experiment"] = module

34	@pytest.fixture

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Per-skill --add-dir experiment for iter-3.1.
3	
4	Tests whether Bucket A3 discovery failures are caused by:
5	- (H1) Plugin skills shadowing marketplace skills in the discovery path
6	- (H2) Marketplace descriptions don't surface in the discovery path
7	- (H3) Choice paralysis from 26+ marketplace skills in scope
8	
9	Method: for each Bucket A3 eval, run the with-skill config TWO ways:
10	1. CONTROL: full marketplace in scope (matches iter-2 behavior)
11	2. TREATMENT: only the EXPECTED marketplace skill in scope (no plugins)
12	
13	If treatment lifts and control doesn't, the issue is choice paralysis
14	(H3) or plugin shadowing (H1) -- NOT description content (H2).
15	"""
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import json
19	import subprocess
20	import sys
21	from pathlib import Path
22	
23	REPO = Path("/Users/felix/Documents/AutoPluginClaw/taches-principled-light")
24	WORKSPACE = REPO / "docs/principled/skill-evals/marketplace-routing-2026-06-22"
25	OUT_DIR = WORKSPACE / "iteration-3.1"
26	EVALS_FILE = WORKSPACE / "evals/evals.json"

38	
39	def preflight() -> bool:

50	def run_one(utterance: str, with_skill: bool, eval_dir: Path,
51	            skill_only: bool = False, skill_dir: str | None = None) -> dict:
52	    cmd = [CLAUDE, "--print",
53	           "--output-format", "stream-json",
54	           "--model", CLAUDE_MODEL,
55	           "--dangerously-skip-permissions"]
56	    if with_skill:

62	        EMPTY_PROJECT = Path("/tmp/empty-claude-project")

66	    # Note: claude CLI requires stdin for the prompt when using --print;
67	    # passing it as the final cmd arg doesn't work (verified empirically).
68	
69	    try:
70	        r = subprocess.run(cmd, input=utterance, capture_output=True, text=True,
71	                           timeout=TIMEOUT_S, cwd=add_dir)
72	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
73	        eval_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
74	        marker = eval_dir / "timeout.json"
75	        marker.write_text(json.dumps({
76	            "status": "timeout",
77	            "timeout_s": TIMEOUT_S,
78	            "duration_ms": TIMEOUT_S * 1000,
79	        }))
80	        return {"status": "timeout", "duration_ms": TIMEOUT_S * 1000,

82	    eval_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

89	        "duration_ms": 0,
90	        "total_events": sum(1 for line in r.stdout.splitlines() if line.strip()),
91	        "stderr_tail": (r.stderr or "")[-500:],

94	
95	def main() -> int:
96	    OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
97	    evals_data = json.loads(EVALS_FILE.read_text())
98	    all_evals = {e["id"]: e for e in evals_data["evals"]}

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Iteration-4 Phase A orchestrator.
3	
4	Re-runs iter-2's transcript generation with a FRESH plugin cache (the
5	v2.0.0 cache that contaminated iter-2/3 was cleared on 2026-06-23),
6	then applies iter-3's assertion-based grader to the new transcripts.
7	This produces a direct iter-3 vs iter-4 comparison answering: "Does the
8	+8.69pp mean lift from iter-3 hold when the cache is fresh?"
9	
10	Pipeline:
11	  1. preflight (proxy reachable on haiku)
12	  2. for each eval in evals.json:
13	       - run claude with --add-dir REPO (with_skill)
14	       - run claude with --add-dir /tmp/empty-claude-project (without_skill)
15	       - save NDJSON transcripts to iteration-4/eval-<id>/{with,without}_skill.jsonl
16	       - on timeout: write timeout.json marker (no empty dir)
17	  3. for each eval: invoke iter-3 grader.py on each transcript
18	  4. compute deltas (Tessl-style IF + GC + overall) per iter-3
19	  5. aggregate into iteration-4/benchmark.{json,md}
20	
21	Differences from iter-3:
22	  - transcripts regenerated against fresh cache (writes to iteration-4/)
23	  - iter-3 grader reused (assertion bug fixed in commit b45c40a)
24	  - timeout.json marker prevents ambiguous empty-dir state
25	  - heterogeneous judge (sonnet over haiku solver) — same as iter-3 default
26	"""
27	from __future__ import annotations
28	
29	import json
30	import subprocess
31	import sys
32	import time
33	from pathlib import Path
34	
35	REPO = Path("/Users/felix/Documents/AutoPluginClaw/taches-principled-light")
36	WORKSPACE = REPO / "docs/principled/skill-evals/marketplace-routing-2026-06-22"
37	ITER4_DIR = WORKSPACE / "iteration-4"
38	ITER3_ASSERTIONS = (WORKSPACE / "iteration-3") / "assertions"
39	ITER3_GRADER = (WORKSPACE / "iteration-3") / "scripts" / "grader.py"
40	EVALS_FILE = WORKSPACE / "evals/evals.json"
41	CLAUDE = "/Users/felix/.local/bin/claude"
42	CLAUDE_MODEL = "haiku"
43	TIMEOUT_S = 300
44	EMPTY_PROJECT = Path("/tmp/empty-claude-project")
45	DEFAULT_JUDGE_MODEL = "sonnet"
46	LIFT_THRESHOLD_PP = 5
47	
48	
49	def preflight() -> bool:
50	    print(f"[iter-4] preflight: {CLAUDE} --model {CLAUDE_MODEL}", flush=True)
51	    EMPTY_PROJECT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
52	    cmd = [CLAUDE, "--print", "--output-format", "stream-json",
53	           "--model", CLAUDE_MODEL, "--dangerously-skip-permissions",
54	           "--add-dir", str(EMPTY_PROJECT)]
55	    try:
56	        r = subprocess.run(cmd, input="echo ok", capture_output=True,
57	                           text=True, timeout=60)
58	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
59	        print("[iter-4] preflight FAIL: 60s timeout", file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
60	        return False
61	    # Look only at the final result envelope, not the init/system events
62	    # which contain tool lists and MCP status that falsely match error
63	    # patterns (e.g. 'chrome-mcp-server failed' is normal).
64	    result_line = ""
65	    for line in (r.stdout or "").splitlines():
66	        if '"type":"result"' in line:
67	            result_line = line
68	            break
69	    if not result_line:
70	        print("[iter-4] preflight FAIL: no result envelope in stream",
71	              file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
72	        return False
73	    is_error = '"is_error":true' in result_line
74	    api_err_status = any(p in result_line for p in
75	                         ["529", "Overloaded", '"api_error_status":5',
76	                          '"api_error_status":4'])
77	    if r.returncode != 0 or is_error or api_err_status:
78	        print(f"[iter-4] preflight FAIL: rc={r.returncode} "
79	              f"is_error={is_error} api_err={api_err_status}",
80	              file=sys.stderr, flush=True)
81	        print(result_line[:300], file=sys.stderr)
82	        return False
83	    print("[iter-4] preflight OK", flush=True)
84	    return True
85	
86	
87	def run_one(utterance: str, with_skill: bool, eval_dir: Path) -> dict:
88	    cfg = "with" if with_skill else "without"
89	    add_dir = str(REPO) if with_skill else str(EMPTY_PROJECT)
90	    if not with_skill:
91	        EMPTY_PROJECT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
92	    out_path = eval_dir / f"{cfg}_skill.jsonl"
93	    log_path = eval_dir / f"{cfg}_skill.log"
94	    cmd = [CLAUDE, "--print", "--output-format", "stream-json",
95	           "--model", CLAUDE_MODEL, "--add-dir", add_dir,
96	           "--dangerously-skip-permissions"]
97	    start = time.time()
98	    try:
99	        with open(out_path, "wb") as fb:
100	            r = subprocess.run(cmd, input=utterance.encode("utf-8"),
101	                               stdout=fb, stderr=subprocess.PIPE,
102	                               timeout=TIMEOUT_S, cwd=add_dir)
103	        duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
104	        log_path.write_text(r.stderr.decode("utf-8", errors="replace"))
105	        status = "completed" if r.returncode == 0 else f"exit_{r.returncode}"
106	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
107	        duration_ms = TIMEOUT_S * 1000
108	        log_path.write_text(f"TIMEOUT after {TIMEOUT_S}s\n")
109	        marker = eval_dir / f"{cfg}_skill_timeout.json"
110	        marker.write_text(json.dumps({
111	            "status": "timeout", "timeout_s": TIMEOUT_S,
112	            "timestamp": time.time(),
113	        }))
114	        status = "truncated_timeout"
115	    text = out_path.read_text() if out_path.exists() else ""
116	    n_events = sum(1 for ln in text.splitlines() if ln.strip())
117	    return {"config": cfg, "status": status, "duration_ms": duration_ms,
118	            "total_events": n_events}
119	
120	
121	def grade_one(eval_id: str, config: str, transcript: Path,
122	              out_dir: Path, judge_model: str) -> dict | None:
123	    assertions_path = ITER3_ASSERTIONS / f"{eval_id}.json"
124	    if not assertions_path.exists():
125	        print(f"[iter-4] {eval_id}: no assertions at {assertions_path}",
126	              file=sys.stderr)
127	        return None
128	    if not transcript.exists() or transcript.stat().st_size == 0:
129	        print(f"[iter-4] {eval_id} {config}: empty/missing transcript",
130	              file=sys.stderr)
131	        return None
132	    cmd = [sys.executable, str(ITER3_GRADER),
133	           "--assertions", str(assertions_path),
134	           "--transcript", str(transcript),
135	           "--eval-dir", str(out_dir),
136	           "--config", config,
137	           "--judge-model", judge_model]
138	    try:
139	        r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=300)
140	    except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
141	        print(f"[iter-4] {eval_id} {config}: grader TIMEOUT", file=sys.stderr)
142	        return None
143	    if r.returncode != 0:
144	        print(f"[iter-4] {eval_id} {config}: grader rc={r.returncode}",
145	              file=sys.stderr)
146	        print((r.stderr or "")[:300], file=sys.stderr)
147	        return None
148	    out = out_dir / f"grading_{config}_skill.json"
149	    return json.loads(out.read_text()) if out.exists() else None
150	
151	
152	def compute_deltas(with_g: dict, without_g: dict) -> dict:
153	    s_w = with_g["summary"]
154	    s_wo = without_g["summary"]
155	    if_d = round(s_w["instruction_following_score"]
156	                 - s_wo["instruction_following_score"], 1)
157	    gc_d = round(s_w["goal_completion_score"]
158	                 - s_wo["goal_completion_score"], 1)
159	    overall_d = round(s_w["overall_score"] - s_wo["overall_score"], 1)
160	    if abs(overall_d) <= LIFT_THRESHOLD_PP:
161	        verdict = "skill_neutral"
162	    elif overall_d < -LIFT_THRESHOLD_PP:
163	        verdict = "skill_hurts"
164	    elif if_d <= 1 and gc_d <= 1:
165	        verdict = "skill_redundant"
166	    else:
167	        verdict = "skill_lifts_quality" if overall_d > 0 else "skill_neutral"
168	    return {
169	        "with_skill": {k: s_w[k] for k in
170	                       ("overall_score", "instruction_following_score",
171	                        "goal_completion_score", "passed", "failed",
172	                        "unknown", "total")},
173	        "without_skill": {k: s_wo[k] for k in
174	                          ("overall_score", "instruction_following_score",
175	                           "goal_completion_score", "passed", "failed",
176	                           "unknown", "total")},
177	        "delta": {"instruction_following_delta": if_d,
178	                  "goal_completion_delta": gc_d,
179	                  "overall_delta": overall_d},
180	        "verdict": verdict,
181	    }
182	
183	
184	def main() -> int:
185	    import argparse
186	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
187	    p.add_argument("--judge-model", default=DEFAULT_JUDGE_MODEL)
188	    p.add_argument("--evals", nargs="*", default=None,
189	                   help="Subset of eval IDs; default = all 17")
190	    p.add_argument("--skip-transcripts", action="store_true",
191	                   help="Skip transcript generation (re-use existing)")
192	    args = p.parse_args()
193	
194	    ITER4_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
195	    EMPTY_PROJECT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
196	
197	    evals = json.loads(EVALS_FILE.read_text())["evals"]
198	    if args.evals:
199	        ids = set(args.evals)
200	        evals = [e for e in evals if e["id"] in ids]
201	    n = len(evals)
202	    print(f"[iter-4] {n} evals x 2 configs = {n*2} transcript runs", flush=True)
203	    print(f"[iter-4] judge model: {args.judge_model}", flush=True)
204	    print(f"[iter-4] timeout per run: {TIMEOUT_S}s", flush=True)
205	    print(f"[iter-4] wall budget: ~{n*2*TIMEOUT_S//60} min worst case",
206	          flush=True)
207	
208	    if not args.skip_transcripts and not preflight():
209	        print("[iter-4] abort: preflight failed", file=sys.stderr)
210	        return 3
211	
212	    # Phase 1: transcript generation
213	    if not args.skip_transcripts:
214	        for i, ev in enumerate(evals, 1):
215	            eval_id = ev["id"]
216	            utterance = ev["utterance"]
217	            ed = ITER4_DIR / f"eval-{eval_id}"
218	            ed.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
219	            print(f"\n[{i}/{n}] {eval_id} (expected={ev['expected']})",
220	                  flush=True)
221	            print(f"  utterance: {utterance}", flush=True)
222	            m_w = run_one(utterance, with_skill=True, eval_dir=ed)
223	            print(f"  with-skill:    {m_w['status']} "
224	                  f"dur={m_w['duration_ms']}ms events={m_w['total_events']}",
225	                  flush=True)
226	            m_wo = run_one(utterance, with_skill=False, eval_dir=ed)
227	            print(f"  without-skill: {m_wo['status']} "
228	                  f"dur={m_wo['duration_ms']}ms events={m_wo['total_events']}",
229	                  flush=True)
230	            (ed / "transcript_metadata.json").write_text(json.dumps({
231	                "eval_id": eval_id, "with_skill": m_w,
232	                "without_skill": m_wo}, indent=2))
233	    else:
234	        print("[iter-4] --skip-transcripts: reusing existing NDJSON",
235	              flush=True)
236	
237	    # Phase 2: grading
238	    print(f"\n[iter-4] grading phase ({n} evals x 2 configs = {n*2} calls)",
239	          flush=True)
240	    all_cmp = []
241	    for i, ev in enumerate(evals, 1):
242	        eval_id = ev["id"]
243	        ed = ITER4_DIR / f"eval-{eval_id}"
244	        print(f"\n[{i}/{n}] grading {eval_id}", flush=True)
245	        g_w = grade_one(eval_id, "with", ed / "with_skill.jsonl",
246	                        ed, args.judge_model)
247	        if g_w:
248	            s = g_w["summary"]
249	            print(f"  with-skill:    overall={s['overall_score']} "
250	                  f"IF={s['instruction_following_score']} "
251	                  f"GC={s['goal_completion_score']}", flush=True)
252	        g_wo = grade_one(eval_id, "without", ed / "without_skill.jsonl",
253	                         ed, args.judge_model)
254	        if g_wo:
255	            s = g_wo["summary"]
256	            print(f"  without-skill: overall={s['overall_score']} "
257	                  f"IF={s['instruction_following_score']} "
258	                  f"GC={s['goal_completion_score']}", flush=True)
259	        if not (g_w and g_wo):
260	            print(f"  SKIP {eval_id} (missing grading)", file=sys.stderr)
261	            continue
262	        deltas = compute_deltas(g_w, g_wo)
263	        cmp_entry = {
264	            "eval_id": eval_id, "utterance": ev["utterance"],
265	            "expected_skill": ev["expected"],
266	            "category_class": ev.get("category", "?"), **deltas,
267	        }
268	        (ed / "comparison.json").write_text(json.dumps(cmp_entry, indent=2))
269	        d = deltas["delta"]
270	        print(f"  delta: IF={d['instruction_following_delta']:+5.1f} "
271	              f"GC={d['goal_completion_delta']:+5.1f} "
272	              f"overall={d['overall_delta']:+5.1f} → {deltas['verdict']}",
273	              flush=True)
274	        all_cmp.append(cmp_entry)
275	
276	    if not all_cmp:
277	        print("[iter-4] no comparisons produced", file=sys.stderr)
278	        return 1
279	
280	    # Phase 3: aggregate
281	    n_done = len(all_cmp)
282	    mean_if = round(sum(c["delta"]["instruction_following_delta"]
283	                        for c in all_cmp) / n_done, 2)
284	    mean_gc = round(sum(c["delta"]["goal_completion_delta"]
285	                        for c in all_cmp) / n_done, 2)
286	    mean_ov = round(sum(c["delta"]["overall_delta"]
287	                        for c in all_cmp) / n_done, 2)
288	    verdicts: dict[str, int] = {}
289	    for c in all_cmp:
290	        verdicts[c["verdict"]] = verdicts.get(c["verdict"], 0) + 1
291	    per_skill: dict[str, list[float]] = {}
292	    for c in all_cmp:
293	        per_skill.setdefault(c["expected_skill"], []).append(
294	            c["delta"]["overall_delta"])
295	    per_skill_mean = {s: round(sum(v)/len(v), 2) for s, v in per_skill.items()}
296	
297	    bench = {
298	        "iteration": 4, "phase": "A",
299	        "scope": f"{n_done} of {n} evals",
300	        "judge_model": args.judge_model,
301	        "solver_model": CLAUDE_MODEL,
302	        "cache_state": "fresh (cleared 2026-06-23, smoke test passed)",
303	        "results": all_cmp,
304	        "summary": {
305	            "total_evals": n_done,
306	            "mean_instruction_following_delta": mean_if,
307	            "mean_goal_completion_delta": mean_gc,
308	            "mean_overall_delta": mean_ov,
309	            "verdict_distribution": verdicts,
310	            "per_skill_lift": per_skill_mean,
311	        },
312	        "iter3_reference": {
313	            "mean_overall_delta": 8.69,
314	            "verdict_distribution": {
315	                "skill_lifts_quality": 6, "skill_neutral": 11,
316	                "skill_hurts": 0},
317	            "caveat": "iter-3 was contaminated by stale v2.0.0 plugin cache",
318	        },
319	        "notes": (
320	            "Phase A: cache-refreshed re-run of iter-3 with heterogeneous "
321	            "judge (sonnet over haiku solver). Transcripts regenerated "
322	            f"against fresh cache; iter-3 grader.py reused (commit b45c40a "
323	            "consultation fix)."),
324	    }
325	    (ITER4_DIR / "benchmark.json").write_text(json.dumps(bench, indent=2))
326	
327	    md = [
328	        f"# Iteration 4 Phase A — Cache-Refreshed Re-Run (N={n_done})",
329	        "",
330	        "## Configuration",
331	        f"- Judge model: `{args.judge_model}`",
332	        f"- Solver model: `{CLAUDE_MODEL}`",
333	        f"- Cache state: fresh (cleared 2026-06-23)",
334	        f"- Lift threshold: ±{LIFT_THRESHOLD_PP}pp for skill_neutral",
335	        "",
336	        "## Summary",
337	        f"- Evals graded: {n_done}/{n}",
338	        f"- Mean IF delta: {mean_if:+.2f}",
339	        f"- Mean GC delta: {mean_gc:+.2f}",
340	        f"- Mean overall delta: {mean_ov:+.2f}",
341	        f"- iter-3 reference: +8.69pp mean overall delta",
342	        "",
343	        "### Verdict distribution",
344	    ]
345	    for v, c in sorted(verdicts.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
346	        md.append(f"- `{v}`: {c}/{n_done}")
347	    md.extend([
348	        "", "### Per-skill mean overall delta", "",
349	        "| Skill | Mean Δ | Evals |",
350	        "|-------|-------:|------:|",
351	    ])
352	    for s, lift in sorted(per_skill_mean.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
353	        md.append(f"| {s} | {lift:+.2f} | {len(per_skill[s])} |")
354	    md.extend([
355	        "", "## Per-eval results", "",
356	        "| Eval | Expected | IF Δ | GC Δ | Overall Δ | Verdict |",
357	        "|------|----------|-----:|-----:|----------:|---------|",
358	    ])
359	    for c in all_cmp:
360	        d = c["delta"]
361	        md.append(f"| {c['eval_id']} | {c['expected_skill']} | "
362	                  f"{d['instruction_following_delta']:+5.1f} | "
363	                  f"{d['goal_completion_delta']:+5.1f} | "
364	                  f"{d['overall_delta']:+5.1f} | {c['verdict']} |")
365	    md.append("")
366	    (ITER4_DIR / "benchmark.md").write_text("\n".join(md))
367	
368	    print(f"\n[iter-4] done. {n_done}/{n} evals graded.")
369	    print(f"[iter-4] mean overall delta: {mean_ov:+.2f} "
370	          f"(iter-3 ref: +8.69)")
371	    print(f"[iter-4] verdicts: {verdicts}")
372	    return 0
373	
374	
375	if __name__ == "__main__":
376	    sys.exit(main())
